page: Microsoft Azure E16as_v6 is an Eav6-family virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz and is supported by 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance has no local storage bundled but is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Text processing performance is also highly efficient, ranking in the top 10% of Geekbench tests. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for small-block cached reads and large-block writes, though large-block uncached read bandwidth is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Small-model LLM text generation is strong, while large-model prompt processing is poor.

Given its high memory density of 8.0 GB per vCPU and dedicated CPU allocation, the E16as_v6 offers a balanced cost-to-resource profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local storage or GPU acceleration. The combination of strong single-core performance and substantial memory capacity makes this server type suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers, text processing pipelines, and enterprise applications requiring a high memory footprint relative to compute resources.
description: Microsoft Azure E16as_v6 is an Eav6-family virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, utilizing an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. This x86_64 architecture instance features 8 physical cores with multithreading, 32 MB of L3 cache, and is Premium Storage capable, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU and text processing capabilities, alongside strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is weak, and multi-core metrics remain average. With an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance provides a cost-effective profile for memory-optimized workloads. It is well-suited for database operations, text processing, in-memory caching, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring significant memory capacity without the need for local NVMe drives.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16as_v6 is an Eav6-family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16as_v6 is an Eav6 VM with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
tagline: An x86 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, offering an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Suited for databases, in-memory caching, and text processing pipelines
